bookStructure.note: chapterCount counts the local generated references/source-notes.md source wrappers. The table of contents describes 7 book chapters plus 3 appendices.

distillationMethod.method: book2skill RIA-TV++ adapted to a single executable Codex skill
distillationMethod.stage0: Adler-style whole-book understanding from companion source set: structure, interpretation, critique, application
distillationMethod.stage1: Five extraction passes recorded under candidates/: framework, principles, cases, counterexamples, glossary
distillationMethod.stage1_5: Triple verification: cross-source support, predictive utility, non-obviousness
distillationMethod.stage2: RIA++ skill construction with Reading, Interpretation, Past Application, Future Trigger, Execution, Boundary
distillationMethod.stage3: Zettelkasten-style index and relation graph recorded in INDEX.md
distillationMethod.stage4: Trigger, non-trigger, and edge-case prompts recorded in test-prompts.json

keyConcepts: logic as formal structure and inference
ontology as domain categories and commitments
rules, frames, semantic networks, object-oriented systems, SQL, Prolog
processes, events, situations, change, Petri nets
purpose, context, modal reasoning, intentional reasoning, agents
open, closed, and semi-open world assumptions
language patterns and controlled natural language
auditable explanations with provenance and revision conditions

qualityChecks.limitationsRecorded: The local source is companion material rather than full book chapters; this is explicitly stated in SKILL.md and BOOK_OVERVIEW.md.

findings.issue: Execution flow had useful KR method but no explicit activation gate or fixed output contract.
Standalone capability needed stronger assurance that ordinary execution does not require reopening Sowa source pages.
Test prompts covered trigger classes but did not specify behavioral expectations for compliance measurement.

runtimeDependencyPolicy: Closed skill: execute from SKILL.md; use references/source-notes.md only as local optional provenance; do not require external source folders, webpages, original books, source reports, machine-local paths, or cross-skill files.
